title: Sample medium-risk unit: handwashing technique + critical times (source-cited)
context: The handwashing technique + critical times unit is the canonical first medium-risk content unit for wash-guides, serving as the reference example for how to author, cite, and review a WASH how-to. It demonstrates the full authoring workflow — paraphrasing authoritative guidance, citing sources with license verification, writing plain-language steps per the style guide, and completing a domain review with a distinct approver — so all subsequent content tasks can follow the same pattern. It also validates that the content schema (data-003) and style guide (style-005) work in practice.
objective: Author, source-cite, and get domain-reviewed a complete medium-risk WashUnit covering proper handwashing technique (the five-step WHO/CDC method) and the critical times when handwashing is most important (e.g., before eating, after using the toilet, after handling feces), fully conforming to the content schema and style guide.
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim in the unit is traceable to a named authoritative source (WHO, UNICEF, or CDC) with retrieval date and verified source license recorded in sources[]; no claim appears without a citation
All source text is paraphrased — no verbatim copied WHO/UNICEF/CDC text; no third-party logos or emblems reproduced; CI copy/similarity check passes
Steps are plain-language and low-literacy-friendly per the style guide (target reading level met, step length within bounds); CI readability check passes
Unit conforms to the WashUnit schema (data-003): all required fields present, reviewedBy and approvedBy are distinct people (no self-approval), riskClass is medium, no clinical instruction included
Unit is reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author; a PR-tied review-log entry is written recording the review
CI schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, and readability gates all pass on this unit
output: A committed, domain-reviewed, source-cited WashUnit document covering handwashing technique and critical times, conforming to the content schema and style guide, with a PR-tied review-log entry.
